queries.question: Where will user2 go to the concert and who will perform?
queries.sql: SELECT artists.name, concerts.city FROM concerts JOIN tickets ON concerts.id = tickets.concertId JOIN artists ON artists.id = concerts.artistId JOIN bookings ON bookings.ticketId = tickets.id  JOIN users ON bookings.userId = users.id WHERE users.username = "user2";
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/entertainment#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/entertainment/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?name ?city WHERE {
  ?concerts a :Concert .
  ?tickets a :Tickets .
  ?tickets p:hasconcerts ?concerts .
  ?artists a :Artist .
  ?concerts p:hasartists ?artists .
  ?bookings a :bookings .
  ?bookings p:hastickets ?tickets .
  ?users a :users .
  ?bookings p:hasusers ?users .
  ?artists p:name ?name .
  ?concerts p:city ?city .
  ?users p:username ?username_f .
  FILTER(STR(?username_f) = "user2")
}

queries.question: Where will user2 go to the concert and who will perform?
queries.sql: SELECT artists.name, concerts.city, concertHall FROM concerts JOIN tickets ON concerts.id = tickets.concertId JOIN artists ON artists.id = concerts.artistId JOIN bookings ON bookings.ticketId = tickets.id  JOIN users ON bookings.userId = users.id WHERE users.username = "user2";
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/entertainment#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/entertainment/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?name ?city ?concerthall WHERE {
  ?concerts a :Concert .
  ?tickets a :Tickets .
  ?tickets p:hasconcerts ?concerts .
  ?artists a :Artist .
  ?concerts p:hasartists ?artists .
  ?bookings a :bookings .
  ?bookings p:hastickets ?tickets .
  ?users a :users .
  ?bookings p:hasusers ?users .
  ?artists p:name ?name .
  ?concerts p:city ?city .
  ?concerts p:concertHall ?concerthall .
  ?users p:username ?username_f .
  FILTER(STR(?username_f) = "user2")
}

queries.question: Using the number of tickets as the criteria for popularity, list the top 3 most popular concert dates and their concert halls.
queries.sql: SELECT c.date, c.concertHall FROM `concerts` c JOIN `tickets` t ON c.id = t.concertId GROUP BY c.id ORDER BY SUM(t.quantity) DESC LIMIT 3;
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/entertainment#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/entertainment/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?date ?concerthall WHERE {
  ?c a :Concert .
  ?t a :Tickets .
  ?t p:hasconcerts ?c .
  ?c p:id ?id_g .
  ?c p:date ?date .
  ?c p:concertHall ?concerthall .
}
GROUP BY ?id_g ?date ?concerthall
LIMIT 3

queries.question: Using the number of tickets as the criteria for popularity, list the top 3 most popular concert dates and their cities.
queries.sql: SELECT c.date, c.city FROM `concerts` c JOIN `tickets` t ON c.id = t.concertId GROUP BY c.id ORDER BY SUM(t.quantity) DESC LIMIT 3;
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/entertainment#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/entertainment/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?date ?city WHERE {
  ?c a :Concert .
  ?t a :Tickets .
  ?t p:hasconcerts ?c .
  ?c p:id ?id_g .
  ?c p:date ?date .
  ?c p:city ?city .
}
GROUP BY ?id_g ?date ?city
LIMIT 3

queries.question: List all the concert dates, cities, concert halls, types of ticket and the total number of tickets sold, sorted by highest ticket sales.
queries.sql: SELECT c.date, c.city, concertHall, t.type AS ticket_type, SUM(t.quantity) as total_tickets_sold FROM `concerts` c JOIN `tickets` t ON c.id = t.concertId GROUP BY c.id ORDER BY SUM(t.quantity) DESC;
queries.sparql:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/entertainment#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/entertainment/prop#>
SELECT ?date ?city ?hall ?type (SUM(?q) AS ?tot) WHERE { ?t a :Tickets ; p:type ?type ; p:quantity ?q ; p:hasconcerts ?c . ?c a :Concert ; p:date ?date ; p:city ?city ; p:concertHall ?hall } GROUP BY ?c ?date ?city ?hall ?type
queries.note: override OK (NL-intent GT): SELECT non-grouped t.type with GROUP BY c.id; NL = per concert+ticket-type ticke

queries.question: Using the number of tickets as the criteria for popularity, list the top 3 most popular concert dates, their cities and their concert halls.
queries.sql: SELECT c.date, c.city, concertHall FROM `concerts` c JOIN `tickets` t ON c.id = t.concertId GROUP BY c.id ORDER BY SUM(t.quantity) DESC LIMIT 3;

PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/entertainment#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/entertainment/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
SELECT ?date ?city ?concerthall WHERE {
  ?c a :Concert .
  ?t a :Tickets .
  ?t p:hasconcerts ?c .
  ?c p:id ?id_g .
  ?c p:date ?date .
  ?c p:city ?city .
  ?c p:concertHall ?concerthall .
}
GROUP BY ?id_g ?date ?city ?concerthall
LIMIT 3

ambiguous_dsl.question: Where will user2 go to the concert and who will perform?
ambiguous_dsl.choice_dsl:
PREFIX : <http://ambrosia.example.org/entertainment#>
PREFIX p: <http://ambrosia.example.org/entertainment/prop#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
CHOICE vague {
  component: { SELECT ?name ?city WHERE {
  ?concerts a :Concert .
  ?tickets a :Tickets .
  ?tickets p:hasconcerts ?concerts .
  ?artists a :Artist .
  ?concerts p:hasartists ?artists .
  ?bookings a :bookings .
  ?bookings p:hastickets ?tickets .
  ?users a :users .
  ?bookings p:hasusers ?users .
  ?artists p:name ?name .
  ?concerts p:city ?city .
  ?users p:username ?username_f .
  FILTER(STR(?username_f) = "user2")
} }
  full: { SELECT ?name ?city ?concerthall WHERE {
  ?concerts a :Concert .
  ?tickets a :Tickets .
  ?tickets p:hasconcerts ?concerts .
  ?artists a :Artist .
  ?concerts p:hasartists ?artists .
  ?bookings a :bookings .
  ?bookings p:hastickets ?tickets .
  ?users a :users .
  ?bookings p:hasusers ?users .
  ?artists p:name ?name .
  ?concerts p:city ?city .
  ?concerts p:concertHall ?concerthall .
  ?users p:username ?username_f .
  FILTER(STR(?username_f) = "user2")
} }
  component_2: { SELECT ?name ?concerthall WHERE {
  ?concerts a :Concert .
  ?tickets a :Tickets .
  ?tickets p:hasconcerts ?concerts .
  ?artists a :Artist .
  ?concerts p:hasartists ?artists .
  ?bookings a :bookings .
  ?bookings p:hastickets ?tickets .
  ?users a :users .
  ?bookings p:hasusers ?users .
  ?artists p:name ?name .
  ?concerts p:concertHall ?concerthall .
  ?users p:username ?username_f .
  FILTER(STR(?username_f) = "user2")
} }
}

ambiguous_dsl.question: Display the top 3 most popular concert dates and locations based on the number of tickets.
List all concert dates, locations, types of tickets available, and the total number of tickets sold, sorted by the highest total number of tickets sold.
